Federal Reserve Watcher · Rate Decision | Date: May 6/7, 2026

Federal Reserve Watcher · Rate Decision | Date: May 6/7, 2026

Probabilistic assessment of the next Federal Reserve policy decision

As of 2026-04-06

Final Decision Engine | Meeting: May 6/7, 2026

Inputs Used: Macro Signal Synthesiser; Fed Tone Decoder; Fed Projections Synthesiser; Beige Book Summary.

  • Macro Signal: HOLD (confidence: MEDIUM) - PCE inflation remains stalled at 2.8% while the labor slack gap is currently neutral and stable (1).
  • Behavioural Feasibility: HOLD-BIASED - Post-March communications emphasize a data-dependent "hold" until disinflation progress resumes (2).
  • Fed Worldview: HOLD - Projections prepare for a "deliberate" balancing of dual-mandate risks as PCE is not expected to reach 2.0% until 2028 (3)(4).
  • Real-Economy Confirmation: CONFIRMS - District reporting shows slight to moderate growth tempered by rising price sensitivity and tariff-driven cost pressures (5).

Divergence (this is signal):

  • Macro says: HOLD
  • Behaviour says: HOLD
  • Fed worldview says: HOLD

Decision Probabilities (sum = 100%):

  • CUT: 15%
  • HOLD: 85%
  • HIKE: 0%

Forward Guidance Bias: NEUTRAL - Maintenance of current data-dependency while monitoring the "extent and timing" of future path shifts (6).

Surprise Risk: MEDIUM - Driven by a dissenting minority (Miran) for a cut (4) and earlier dovish assessments regarding labor "fragility" (6).

Final Call: Outcome: HOLD with neutral guidance.

All views expressed are personal, based on publicly available information, and do not represent the views of any employer or reflect any proprietary or internal analysis. This information should not be relied upon for making investment decisions.
